name: Eggplant Au Gratin
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Peel the eggplant and cut the flesh into cubes (about 1 inch), then drop them into boiling salted water. Cook for about 5 minutes, just until cooked through. Drain well.
3. Meanwhile, slice the mushrooms. There should be about 2 cups. Heat 1 tablespoon butter in a skillet and add the mushroom slices. Sprinkle with salt and about 1 teaspoon lemon juice. Cook, stirring and tossing, until the mushrooms give up their juice. Continue cooking until the liquid evaporates. Set aside.
4. Melt 1 1/2 tablespoons butter in a saucepan and add the flour, stirring with a wire whisk. Add the milk and cream, stirring rapidly with the whisk. When blended and smooth, add salt and pepper to taste, the remaining lemon juice, nutmeg, and Tabasco to taste. Stir in the mushrooms and eggplant. Stir in the egg. Spoon the mixture into a baking dish (an 8-inch pie plate works well). Sprinkle with a mixture of crumbs and cheese and dot the remaining tablespoon butter. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes, then brown under the broiler.
